  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Project Officer",
  "description": "The PATH4AIPOCUS Project is a three-year initiative funded by the Gates Foundation. The project aligns with national and global Maternal, Newborn, Child, and Nutrition Health (MNCNH) priorities by supporting the development, validation, and scale-up of Artificial Intelligence&ndash;enabled Point-of-Care Ultrasound (AI-POCUS) within antenatal care (ANC) services in Nigeria.

The project aims to generate robust evidence on health system readiness&mdash;including service delivery regulations, policy alignment, competency frameworks, financing mechanisms, and implementation feasibility&mdash;to inform sustainable integration of AI-enabled POCUS into routine maternal healthcare in resource-constrained settings.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Benin, Edo
Employment Type: Contract

&nbsp;Position Summary


	The Project Officer (Desk Officer) will provide technical and operational support for the PATH4AIPOCUS grant. Working under the supervision of the project coordinator and reporting to the Project Director, the Project Officer will support project design, implementation, monitoring, reporting, proposal development, and stakeholder coordination.
	He will be the chief logistics officer of the grant and also be responsible for maintain the project office, keeping records and arranging various meetings and engagements.
	The ideal candidate will demonstrate strong managerial skills, analytical thinking, proposal implementation capacity, and the ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.


Key&nbsp;Responsibilities&nbsp;
Technical &amp; Research Support:


	Provide technical support to project teams across ongoing research and implementation initiatives.
	Conduct desk reviews, and background research to inform project design and reporting.
	Utilize appropriate statistical and qualitative software (e.g., SPSS, Stata, R, NVivo, Atlas.ti, Dedoose, Power BI) to analyze and interpret data.
	Contribute to high-quality technical reports and policy briefs.
	Monitor the achievements of deliverables


Project Implementation &amp; Monitoring:


	Support implementation of project activities, including coordination and logistics.
	Monitor project milestones and deliverables to ensure alignment with donor expectations.
	Assist in tracking key performance indicators and reporting progress against targets.
	Ensure proper documentation and record-keeping for compliance and audit purposes.
	Arrange the meetings and prepare minutes of such meetings


Stakeholder &amp; Partner Coordination:


	Support the Project Lead in liaising with implementing partners and research collaborators.
	Participate in technical meetings and contribute to strategic and operational planning processes.
	Facilitate smooth collaboration between internal teams and external stakeholders.
	Contribute to brainstorming and idea generation for the smooth running of the grant


Communications &amp; Content Strategy Support:


	Support the planning and execution of digital content strategies across professional platforms (e.g., LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Instagram).
	Assist in drafting engaging content, monitoring audience engagement, and analyzing performance metrics.
	Collaborate with cross-functional teams to maintain consistent brand messaging and enhance organizational visibility.


Organizational Support:


	Participate actively in team meetings and contribute to strategic planning discussions.
	Ensure alignment between project activities, institutional priorities, and donor requirements.
	Perform other duties as assigned by the Technical Manager or Chief Executive Officer.


&nbsp;Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s or Master&rsquo;s degree in Public Health, Social Sciences, Economics, Statistics, Development Studies, computer science or other related discipline.
	Minimum of 2&ndash;4 years of experience in research, project implementation, or development programming.
	Demonstrated experience in data analysis and technical report writing.
	Experience supporting donor-funded projects is an advantage.
	Deep computer knowledge and use of artificial intelligence is an added advantage.


&nbsp;Competencies:


	Strong analytical and critical thinking skills.
	Proficiency in research methods (qualitative and quantitative).
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines.
	Strong interpersonal and teamwork skills.
	High level of professionalism, initiative, and accountability.


Performance Expectations:
Performance will be assessed based on:


	Quality and timeliness of deliverables.
	Contribution to successful grant timelines.
	Grant reporting integrity and adherence to reporting standards.
	Initiative in supporting organizational growth and innovation.

Project Officer Job at PATH4AIPOCUS Project




        
    

        
        The PATH4AIPOCUS Project is a three-year initiative funded by the Gates Foundation. The project aligns with national and global Maternal, Newborn, Child, and Nutrition Health (MNCNH) priorities by supporting the development, validation, and scale-up of Artificial Intelligence–enabled Point-of-Care Ultrasound (AI-POCUS) within antenatal care (ANC) services in Nigeria.

The project aims to generate robust evidence on health system readiness—including service delivery regulations, policy alignment, competency frameworks, financing mechanisms, and implementation feasibility—to inform sustainable integration of AI-enabled POCUS into routine maternal healthcare in resource-constrained settings.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Position: Project Officer

Job Location: Benin, Edo
Employment Type: Contract

 Position Summary


	The Project Officer (Desk Officer) will provide technical and operational support for the PATH4AIPOCUS grant. Working under the supervision of the project coordinator and reporting to the Project Director, the Project Officer will support project design, implementation, monitoring, reporting, proposal development, and stakeholder coordination.
	He will be the chief logistics officer of the grant and also be responsible for maintain the project office, keeping records and arranging various meetings and engagements.
	The ideal candidate will demonstrate strong managerial skills, analytical thinking, proposal implementation capacity, and the ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.


Key Responsibilities 
Technical & Research Support:


	Provide technical support to project teams across ongoing research and implementation initiatives.
	Conduct desk reviews, and background research to inform project design and reporting.
	Utilize appropriate statistical and qualitative software (e.g., SPSS, Stata, R, NVivo, Atlas.ti, Dedoose, Power BI) to analyze and interpret data.
	Contribute to high-quality technical reports and policy briefs.
	Monitor the achievements of deliverables


Project Implementation & Monitoring:


	Support implementation of project activities, including coordination and logistics.
	Monitor project milestones and deliverables to ensure alignment with donor expectations.
	Assist in tracking key performance indicators and reporting progress against targets.
	Ensure proper documentation and record-keeping for compliance and audit purposes.
	Arrange the meetings and prepare minutes of such meetings


Stakeholder & Partner Coordination:


	Support the Project Lead in liaising with implementing partners and research collaborators.
	Participate in technical meetings and contribute to strategic and operational planning processes.
	Facilitate smooth collaboration between internal teams and external stakeholders.
	Contribute to brainstorming and idea generation for the smooth running of the grant


Communications & Content Strategy Support:


	Support the planning and execution of digital content strategies across professional platforms (e.g., LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Instagram).
	Assist in drafting engaging content, monitoring audience engagement, and analyzing performance metrics.
	Collaborate with cross-functional teams to maintain consistent brand messaging and enhance organizational visibility.


Organizational Support:


	Participate actively in team meetings and contribute to strategic planning discussions.
	Ensure alignment between project activities, institutional priorities, and donor requirements.
	Perform other duties as assigned by the Technical Manager or Chief Executive Officer.


 Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Public Health, Social Sciences, Economics, Statistics, Development Studies, computer science or other related discipline.
	Minimum of 2–4 years of experience in research, project implementation, or development programming.
	Demonstrated experience in data analysis and technical report writing.
	Experience supporting donor-funded projects is an advantage.
	Deep computer knowledge and use of artificial intelligence is an added advantage.


 Competencies:


	Strong analytical and critical thinking skills.
	Proficiency in research methods (qualitative and quantitative).
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines.
	Strong interpersonal and teamwork skills.
	High level of professionalism, initiative, and accountability.


Performance Expectations:
Performance will be assessed based on:


	Quality and timeliness of deliverables.
	Contribution to successful grant timelines.
	Grant reporting integrity and adherence to reporting standards.
	Initiative in supporting organizational growth and innovation.

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior Software Engineer",
  "description": "Moniepoint Incorporated is a global business payments and banking platform and recently became QED Investors&rsquo; first investment in Africa. We are the partner of choice for over 600,000 businesses of all sizes, powering the dreams of SMBs and providing them with equal access to the tools they need to grow and scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Software Engineer

Job Location:&nbsp;Nigeria (Remote)

What You&#39;ll Be Doing


	Collaborate with cross-functional teams (product, design, QA) to define, design, and ship high-impact features.
	Design scalable architecture and implement performant backend services using Java and Spring Boot.
	Write clean, maintainable, and well-documented code with a strong focus on reliability and testability.
	Integrate third-party services and APIs while ensuring robust error handling and graceful degradation.
	Continuously improve system performance and developer experience through automation and refactoring.
	Debug complex issues across the stack and proactively identify opportunities for technical improvement.
	Participate in code reviews, architecture discussions, and share best practices with the team.
	Document technical decisions, systems, and processes for future team reference and transparency.


What We&#39;re Looking For


	BS/MS in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field.
	At least 5+ years of professional experience in software engineering, with a strong focus on backend systems.
	Expertise in Java and Spring Boot; solid understanding of modern backend architecture and best practices.
	Experience building and scaling web services, APIs, and distributed systems.
	Familiarity with Agile development methodologies and CI/CD pipelines.
	Proficient with relational databases and ORM frameworks (e.g., Hibernate).
	Exposure to frontend frameworks like AngularJS or React is a plus.
	Strong debugging skills and a methodical approach to problem-solving.
	Excellent communication skills and a team-first attitude.


Our Tech Stack
As part of our engineering team, you&rsquo;ll work with modern technologies and tools that enable us to build scalable, reliable, and high-performing systems. Some of the technologies you&rsquo;ll be hands-on with include


	Java (latest versions): Powering our core backend systems with a focus on performance and maintainability.
	Spring &amp; Spring Boot: Building modular, production-grade microservices with robust dependency management.
	Docker &amp; Kubernetes: Containerized deployments and orchestration for consistent, scalable environments across dev, staging, and production.
	PostgreSQL &amp; DynamoDB: A combination of relational and NoSQL databases to handle structured data and dynamic scaling needs.
	Elasticsearch: For fast, flexible full-text search and analytics across large datasets.
	AWS Cloud Infrastructure: Deploying and managing microservices using a cloud-native architecture that supports global scalability.


What We Can Offer You


	Culture - We put our people first and prioritize the well-being of every team member. We&rsquo;ve built a company where all opinions carry weight and where all voices are heard. We value and respect each other and always look out for one another. Above all, we are human.
	Learning - We have a learning and development-focused environment with an emphasis on knowledge sharing, training, and regular internal technical talks.
	Compensation - You&rsquo;ll receive an attractive salary, pension, health insurance, annual bonus, plus other benefits.


What to expect in the hiring process
Our interview process is designed to be thoughtful, transparent, and candidate-friendly, allowing you to showcase your strengths while getting to know us better:


	Initial Conversation:&nbsp;A brief introductory call with our recruiter to learn more about your background, career goals, and to share insights about the role and our team.
	Technical Assessment: A practical, take-home coding exercise that reflects real-world challenges. You&#39;ll have ample time to complete this in your own environment
	Deep Dive Technical Interview: A focused session with our Engineering Team, where we&#39;ll explore system design, architecture, and problem-solving approaches.
	Final Interview: A combined technical and behavioural conversation with a member of our Executive Team, aimed at understanding your alignment with our values, vision, and culture.

Senior Software Engineer Job at Moniepoint




        
    

        
        Moniepoint Incorporated is a global business payments and banking platform and recently became QED Investors’ first investment in Africa. We are the partner of choice for over 600,000 businesses of all sizes, powering the dreams of SMBs and providing them with equal access to the tools they need to grow and scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Software Engineer

Job Location: Nigeria (Remote)

What You'll Be Doing

	Collaborate with cross-functional teams (product, design, QA) to define, design, and ship high-impact features.
	Design scalable architecture and implement performant backend services using Java and Spring Boot.
	Write clean, maintainable, and well-documented code with a strong focus on reliability and testability.
	Integrate third-party services and APIs while ensuring robust error handling and graceful degradation.
	Continuously improve system performance and developer experience through automation and refactoring.
	Debug complex issues across the stack and proactively identify opportunities for technical improvement.
	Participate in code reviews, architecture discussions, and share best practices with the team.
	Document technical decisions, systems, and processes for future team reference and transparency.


What We're Looking For


	BS/MS in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field.
	At least 5+ years of professional experience in software engineering, with a strong focus on backend systems.
	Expertise in Java and Spring Boot; solid understanding of modern backend architecture and best practices.
	Experience building and scaling web services, APIs, and distributed systems.
	Familiarity with Agile development methodologies and CI/CD pipelines.
	Proficient with relational databases and ORM frameworks (e.g., Hibernate).
	Exposure to frontend frameworks like AngularJS or React is a plus.
	Strong debugging skills and a methodical approach to problem-solving.
	Excellent communication skills and a team-first attitude.


Our Tech Stack
As part of our engineering team, you’ll work with modern technologies and tools that enable us to build scalable, reliable, and high-performing systems. Some of the technologies you’ll be hands-on with include


	Java (latest versions): Powering our core backend systems with a focus on performance and maintainability.
	Spring & Spring Boot: Building modular, production-grade microservices with robust dependency management.
	Docker & Kubernetes: Containerized deployments and orchestration for consistent, scalable environments across dev, staging, and production.
	PostgreSQL & DynamoDB: A combination of relational and NoSQL databases to handle structured data and dynamic scaling needs.
	Elasticsearch: For fast, flexible full-text search and analytics across large datasets.
	AWS Cloud Infrastructure: Deploying and managing microservices using a cloud-native architecture that supports global scalability.


What We Can Offer You


	Culture - We put our people first and prioritize the well-being of every team member. We’ve built a company where all opinions carry weight and where all voices are heard. We value and respect each other and always look out for one another. Above all, we are human.
	Learning - We have a learning and development-focused environment with an emphasis on knowledge sharing, training, and regular internal technical talks.
	Compensation - You’ll receive an attractive salary, pension, health insurance, annual bonus, plus other benefits.


What to expect in the hiring process
Our interview process is designed to be thoughtful, transparent, and candidate-friendly, allowing you to showcase your strengths while getting to know us better:


	Initial Conversation: A brief introductory call with our recruiter to learn more about your background, career goals, and to share insights about the role and our team.
	Technical Assessment: A practical, take-home coding exercise that reflects real-world challenges. You'll have ample time to complete this in your own environment
	Deep Dive Technical Interview: A focused session with our Engineering Team, where we'll explore system design, architecture, and problem-solving approaches.
	Final Interview: A combined technical and behavioural conversation with a member of our Executive Team, aimed at understanding your alignment with our values, vision, and culture.

  "description": "Moniepoint Incorporated is a global business payments and banking platform and recently became QED Investors&rsquo; first investment in Africa. We are the partner of choice for over 600,000 businesses of all sizes, powering the dreams of SMBs and providing them with equal access to the tools they need to grow and scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Payroll Specialist

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About the role


	The Payroll Specialist provides essential, high-precision support in delivering timely and accurate payroll across Moniepoint&rsquo;s global markets. you will ensure that our international workforce is paid accurately and on time.
	You are the guardian of payroll data integrity, a key point of contact for employee queries, and a champion of compliant financial reporting.


Key Responsibilities&nbsp;
Global Payroll Execution &amp; Validation:


	End-to-End Processing: Prepare, validate, and record payroll inputs across all active platforms in multiple countries, ensuring local nuances and regional labor laws are respected.
	Accuracy &amp; Reconciliation: Execute rigorous monthly cycles, including variance reviews, accuracy checks, and reconciliation of payroll feeders to the general ledger.
	HRIS Synchronization: Audit and validate that employee lifecycle changes (hires, terminations, promotions) are mirrored accurately between HRIS and payroll systems.


Compliance, SOX &amp; Statutory Oversight;


	SOX Compliance &amp; Controls: Maintain and adhere to SOX-compliant workflows, ensuring all payroll activities follow established internal controls, approval hierarchies, and strict audit trails.
	Statutory Submissions: Support the timely filing of PAYE, pensions, taxes, and other local statutory requirements across all operating jurisdictions.
	Audit Readiness: Assist in producing detailed schedules and documentation for internal and external audits, ensuring a &quot;zero-findings&quot; environment.


Stakeholder Management &amp; Optimization:


	Employee Experience: Respond to payroll-related inquiries with professional clarity, maintaining high satisfaction through timely and empathetic communication.
	Third-Party Management: Coordinate with payroll providers and third-party vendors to ensure service level agreements (SLAs) are met.
	Process Improvement: Troubleshoot data discrepancies and collaborate with HRIS and Finance teams to automate manual tasks and maintain updated process documentation (SOPs).


Qualifications


	Experience:&nbsp;4 or more years of dedicated payroll or HR operations experience in a professional environment.
	Systems Fluency:&nbsp;Hands-on familiarity with global payroll platforms such as&nbsp;Payfit, Gusto, Monnify, Razorpay, or similar cloud-based tools.
	Analytical Prowess:&nbsp;Strong attention to detail and advanced confidence working with spreadsheets, complex data sets, and financial numbers.


Preferred Qualifications:


	International Scope:&nbsp;Prior experience supporting&nbsp;multi-entity and multi-currency&nbsp;payroll environments.
	Regional Expertise:&nbsp;A basic understanding of payroll requirements across&nbsp;African, UK, or Asian&nbsp;markets.
	Regulatory Knowledge: Familiarity with maintaining internal controls (SOX) within a financial operations context


About You:


	Precise &amp; Dependable:&nbsp;You are organized by nature and take deep pride in &quot;getting it right the first time.&quot;
	Effective Communicator:&nbsp;You translate complex payroll data into clear, helpful support for colleagues and employees alike.
	Growth-Oriented:&nbsp;You are eager to deepen your technical payroll expertise across multiple platforms and stay ahead of global compliance trends.
	Trustworthy:&nbsp;You handle sensitive data with the highest level of confidentiality and professional integrity.

Job Location:&nbsp;Nationwide

Description


	The Customer Care Sales Specialist is responsible for managing customer interactions, resolving inquiries, and promoting company products or services to drive sales.
	The role focuses on delivering excellent customer experience while identifying sales opportunities, maintaining strong client relationships, and supporting business growth.


Key Responsibilities


	Respond promptly to customer inquiries through phone, email, social media, or in-person interactions.
	Provide accurate information about products, services, pricing, and promotions.
	Identify customer needs and recommend suitable products or services.
	Convert customer inquiries into sales opportunities.
	Maintain strong relationships with new and existing customers.
	Handle customer complaints professionally and ensure timely resolution.
	Follow up with customers to ensure satisfaction and encourage repeat business.
	Maintain proper records of customer interactions, transactions, and feedback.
	Meet or exceed assigned sales targets and customer satisfaction goals.
	Collaborate with the sales and marketing teams to improve customer experience.
	Update customer databases and CRM systems with accurate information.

	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or HND qualification in Business Administration, Marketing, Communication, or related fields
	1 year of experience in customer service, sales, or customer relationship management.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Strong persuasion and negotiation abilities.
	Ability to multitask and manage customer interactions effectively.
	Good problem-solving and conflict resolution skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and CRM systems.
	Strong customer service orientation and sales mindset.
